revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = お松大権現 |画像 = [[File:Omatsugongen hall of worship.JPG|300px]] |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]字不ケ63番地 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|54|53.8|N|134|33|9.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神 =（儀）義理大権現（お松、[[仁木義広]]の孫、宅左衛門の女） |神体 =槇の木 |社格 = |創建 = [[江戸時代]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''お松大権現'''（おまつ-だいごんげん）は、[[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]にある[[神社]]である。宗教法人お松権現社が設置。通称「'''猫神さん'''<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8" />」と呼ばれており、日本三大怪猫伝の舞台の一つである。
== 歴史 == [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,1.JPG|thumb|250px|猫の狛犬]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,10.JPG|thumb|250px|境内に植えられている桜]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,13.JPG|thumb|250px|参拝道路]] [[File:Omatsu gongen Shrine office No,2.JPG|thumb|250px|招き猫・御神籤を販売している物販店]] [[江戸時代]]の前期の[[貞享]]年間、[[徳島藩]][[加茂村 (徳島県)|加茂村]]（現・阿南市[[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]）の庄屋が不作である村を救うために[[富豪]]に金を借り、すでに返済したにもかかわらず、富豪の策略で未返済の濡れ衣を着せられ、失意の内に病死した。そこで、[[借金]]の担保になっていた土地は富豪に取り上げられてしまう。庄屋の妻のお松は[[奉行所]]に訴え出るも、富豪に買収された奉行は不当な裁きを下す。お松がそれを不服として藩主に直訴した結果、直訴の罪により[[処刑]]され、お松の飼っていた[[三毛猫]]が[[化け猫]]となり、富豪や奉行らの家を滅ぼしたという伝説に由来する<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=御由緒 |publisher=お松大権現 |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead |url-status-date=2023-02-22}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html| title=お松大権現| publisher=阿南市観光協会 きらりあなん| accessdate=2014-04-05| archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407070625/http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html| archivedate=2014-04-07| url-status=dead|url-status-date=2018-03}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=お松大権現|publisher=徳島県観光情報サイト阿波ナビ | accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=お松大権現|publisher=阿南市 | accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=お松大権現｜合格・必勝祈願の「猫神さま」 |url=https://nekogami.jp/ |website=nekogami.jp |access-date=2023-02-22 |publisher=お松大権現 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230222093117/https://nekogami.jp/ |archive-date=2023-02-22}}</ref>。
お松の死因については、お松のみ処刑<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=阿波のがんこもん |date=2021年1月15日 |publisher=阿波の歴史を小説にする会 |series=阿波の歴史小説 ; 41 |id={{NDLBibID|031210439}} |page=190}}</ref>、お松と猫どちらも処刑<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=ふるさと加茂谷の昔がたり |volume=第1集 |editor=加茂谷郷土研究会 |page=37}}</ref>、自殺<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=阿南の伝説 |date=1976年7月1日 |publisher=小山助学館 |id={{NDLSearch|R100000136-I1130282269463906688}} |page=56}}</ref>をしたという伝承がある。
=== 勝負事に利益がある神社 === 直訴によって悪人を倒したという伝説から、勝負事にも利益があるといわれ、最近ではその勝負事が受験生のものとなっており、[[入学試験|受験シーズン]]には合格祈願の場所として訪れているが、最近では[[失われた20年|平成の長期的な不況]]で、様変わりして本来の目的であるいくつかの勝負事に回帰して[[就職活動]]中の学生・金運上昇を願う人の参拝も増えている。遠隔地在住や用事で訪問不可能な場合には祈祷受付を郵送で行っている<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html| title=お松大権現（阿南市）や王子神社（徳島市） 受験生ら続々参拝| publisher=徳島新聞| accessdate=2014-04-05| archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407095141/http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html| archivedate=2014-04-07| url-status=dead|url-status-date=2018-03}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /><ref name="村上2002">[[#村上2002|村上2002年]]、150-161頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/index.html|title=Home|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/kitou-kigan.html|title=祈祷|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>。また、境内には全国的にも珍しいネコの[[狛犬]]もある。
== 沿革 == * [[江戸時代]] - 開所する。 * [[昭和初期]] - この頃までに、祭神名が義理大権現からお松大権現に改称。 * 1963年 - 本殿が現在の場所に遷宮<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=お松大権現｜合格・必勝祈願の「猫神さん」 |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |website=nekogami.jp |access-date=2023-02-22}}</ref>。
== 祭事 == {| class="wikitable" !内容<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=御祭典|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!開催日<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |お正月祭 |[[1月1日]]から同月3日 |- |お松の命日 |[[旧暦]]の[[3月15日]] |- |御祭典 |[[5月1日]] |- |月並祭 |毎月1日と15日 |}
== 境内社 == [[猫]]にまつわる置物や建物が建てられている。また、1万体以上の招き猫が奉納され、借りて持ち帰ることができる<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/>。 * 招き猫 * 祈願絵馬 * さすり猫 * 資料館 * ジャンボ猫
<gallery> File:Omatsugongen Archives.JPG|招き猫 File:Omatsugongen prayer No,2.JPG|祈祷絵馬 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.914944|134.552722}}|caption=お松大権現|width=256}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=村上健司|title=日本全国妖怪スポット|year=2011|publisher=[[汐文社]]|volume=3|isbn=978-4-8113-8805-2|pages=124|ref={{SfnRef|村上|2011}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[阿南市]] * [[王子神社 (徳島市)]] - こちらの神社もお松とお松の寵愛した猫が祀ってある * [[化け猫#鍋島の化け猫騒動|鍋島の化け猫騒動]] * [[有馬頼義]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Omatsugongen}} * [https://nekogami.jp/ お松大権現]
{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=徳島県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おまつたいこんけん}} [[Category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿南市の歴史]] [[Category:ネコの文化]] [[Category:阿南市の建築物]] [[Category:権現]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = お松大権現 |画像 = [[File:Omatsugongen hall of worship.JPG|300px]] |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]字不ケ63番地 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|54|53.8|N|134|33|9.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神 =（儀）義理大権現（お松、[[仁木義広]]の孫、宅左衛門の女） |神体 =槇の木 |社格 = |創建 = [[江戸時代]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''お松大権現'''（おまつ-だいごんげん）は、[[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]にある[[神社]]である。宗教法人お松権現社が設置。通称「'''猫神さん'''<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8" />」と呼ばれており、日本三大怪猫伝の舞台の一つである。
== 歴史 == [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,1.JPG|thumb|250px|猫の狛犬]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,10.JPG|thumb|250px|境内に植えられている桜]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,13.JPG|thumb|250px|参拝道路]] [[File:Omatsu gongen Shrine office No,2.JPG|thumb|250px|招き猫・御神籤を販売している物販店]] [[江戸時代]]の前期の[[貞享]]年間、[[徳島藩]][[加茂村 (徳島県)|加茂村]]（現・阿南市[[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]）の庄屋が不作である村を救うために[[富豪]]に金を借り、すでに返済したにもかかわらず、富豪の策略で未返済の濡れ衣を着せられ、失意の内に病死した。そこで、[[借金]]の担保になっていた土地は富豪に取り上げられてしまう。庄屋の妻のお松は[[奉行所]]に訴え出るも、富豪に買収された奉行は不当な裁きを下す。お松がそれを不服として藩主に直訴した結果、直訴の罪により[[処刑]]され、お松の飼っていた[[三毛猫]]が[[化け猫]]となり、富豪や奉行らの家を滅ぼしたという伝説に由来する<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=御由緒 |publisher=お松大権現 |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead |url-status-date=2023-02-22}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html| title=お松大権現| publisher=阿南市観光協会 きらりあなん| accessdate=2014-04-05| archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407070625/http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html| archivedate=2014-04-07| url-status=dead|url-status-date=2018-03}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=お松大権現|publisher=徳島県観光情報サイト阿波ナビ | accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=お松大権現|publisher=阿南市 | accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=お松大権現｜合格・必勝祈願の「猫神さま」 |url=https://nekogami.jp/ |website=nekogami.jp |access-date=2023-02-22 |publisher=お松大権現 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230222093117/https://nekogami.jp/ |archive-date=2023-02-22}}</ref>。
お松の死因については、お松のみ処刑<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=阿波のがんこもん |date=2021年1月15日 |publisher=阿波の歴史を小説にする会 |series=阿波の歴史小説 ; 41 |id={{NDLBibID|031210439}} |page=190}}</ref>、お松と猫どちらも処刑<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=ふるさと加茂谷の昔がたり |volume=第1集 |editor=加茂谷郷土研究会 |page=37}}</ref>、自殺<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=阿南の伝説 |date=1976年7月1日 |publisher=小山助学館 |id={{NDLSearch|R100000136-I1130282269463906688}} |page=56}}</ref>をしたという伝承がある。
=== 勝負事に利益がある神社 === 直訴によって悪人を倒したという伝説から、勝負事にも利益があるといわれ、最近ではその勝負事が受験生のものとなっており、[[入学試験|受験シーズン]]には合格祈願の場所として訪れているが、最近では[[失われた20年|平成の長期的な不況]]で、様変わりして本来の目的であるいくつかの勝負事に回帰して[[就職活動]]中の学生・金運上昇を願う人の参拝も増えている。遠隔地在住や用事で訪問不可能な場合には祈祷受付を郵送で行っている<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html| title=お松大権現（阿南市）や王子神社（徳島市） 受験生ら続々参拝| publisher=徳島新聞| accessdate=2014-04-05| archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407095141/http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html| archivedate=2014-04-07| url-status=dead|url-status-date=2018-03}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /><ref name="村上2002">[[#村上2002|村上2002年]]、150-161頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/index.html|title=Home|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/kitou-kigan.html|title=祈祷|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>。また、境内には全国的にも珍しいネコの[[狛犬]]もある。
== 沿革 == * [[江戸時代]] - 開所する。 * [[昭和初期]] - この頃までに、祭神名が義理大権現からお松大権現に改称。 * 1963年 - 本殿が現在の場所に遷宮<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=お松大権現｜合格・必勝祈願の「猫神さん」 |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |website=nekogami.jp |access-date=2023-02-22}}</ref>。
== 祭事 == {| class="wikitable" !内容<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=御祭典|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!開催日<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |お正月祭 |[[1月1日]]から同月3日 |- |お松の命日 |[[旧暦]]の[[3月15日]] |- |御祭典 |[[5月1日]] |- |月並祭 |毎月1日と15日 |}
== 境内社 == [[猫]]にまつわる置物や建物が建てられている。また、1万体以上の招き猫が奉納され、借りて持ち帰ることができる<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/>。 * 招き猫 * 祈願絵馬 * さすり猫 * 資料館 * ジャンボ猫
<gallery> File:Omatsugongen Archives.JPG|招き猫 File:Omatsugongen prayer No,2.JPG|祈祷絵馬 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.914944|134.552722}}|caption=お松大権現|width=256}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=村上健司|title=日本全国妖怪スポット|year=2011|publisher=[[汐文社]]|volume=3|isbn=978-4-8113-8805-2|pages=124|ref={{SfnRef|村上|2011}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[阿南市]] * [[王子神社 (徳島市)]] - こちらの神社もお松とお松の寵愛した猫が祀ってある * [[化け猫#鍋島の化け猫騒動|鍋島の化け猫騒動]] * [[有馬頼義]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Omatsugongen}} * [https://nekogami.jp/ お松大権現]

{{神社 |名称 = お松大権現 |画像 = [[File:Omatsugongen hall of worship.JPG|300px]] |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]字不ケ63番地 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|54|53.8|N|134|33|9.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神 =（儀）義理大権現（お松、[[仁木義広]]の孫、宅左衛門の女） |神体 =槇の木 |社格 = |創建 = [[江戸時代]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''お松大権現'''（おまつ-だいごんげん）は、[[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]にある[[神社]]である。宗教法人お松権現社が設置。通称「'''猫神さん'''<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8" />」と呼ばれており、日本三大怪猫伝の舞台の一つである。
== 歴史 == [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,1.JPG|thumb|250px|猫の狛犬]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,10.JPG|thumb|250px|境内に植えられている桜]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,13.JPG|thumb|250px|参拝道路]] [[File:Omatsu gongen Shrine office No,2.JPG|thumb|250px|招き猫・御神籤を販売している物販店]] [[江戸時代]]の前期の[[貞享]]年間、[[徳島藩]][[加茂村 (徳島県)|加茂村]]（現・阿南市[[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]）の庄屋が不作である村を救うために[[富豪]]に金を借り、すでに返済したにもかかわらず、富豪の策略で未返済の濡れ衣を着せられ、失意の内に病死した。そこで、[[借金]]の担保になっていた土地は富豪に取り上げられてしまう。庄屋の妻のお松は[[奉行所]]に訴え出るも、富豪に買収された奉行は不当な裁きを下す。お松がそれを不服として藩主に直訴した結果、直訴の罪により[[処刑]]され、お松の飼っていた[[三毛猫]]が[[化け猫]]となり、富豪や奉行らの家を滅ぼしたという伝説に由来する<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=御由緒 |publisher=お松大権現 |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead |url-status-date=2023-02-22}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html| title=お松大権現| publisher=阿南市観光協会 きらりあなん| accessdate=2014-04-05| archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407070625/http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html| archivedate=2014-04-07| url-status=dead|url-status-date=2018-03}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=お松大権現|publisher=徳島県観光情報サイト阿波ナビ | accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=お松大権現|publisher=阿南市 | accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=お松大権現｜合格・必勝祈願の「猫神さま」 |url=https://nekogami.jp/ |website=nekogami.jp |access-date=2023-02-22 |publisher=お松大権現 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230222093117/https://nekogami.jp/ |archive-date=2023-02-22}}</ref>。
お松の死因については、お松のみ処刑<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=阿波のがんこもん |date=2021年1月15日 |publisher=阿波の歴史を小説にする会 |series=阿波の歴史小説 ; 41 |id={{NDLBibID|031210439}} |page=190}}</ref>、お松と猫どちらも処刑<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=ふるさと加茂谷の昔がたり |volume=第1集 |editor=加茂谷郷土研究会 |page=37}}</ref>、自殺<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=阿南の伝説 |date=1976年7月1日 |publisher=小山助学館 |id={{NDLSearch|R100000136-I1130282269463906688}} |page=56}}</ref>をしたという伝承がある。
=== 勝負事に利益がある神社 === 直訴によって悪人を倒したという伝説から、勝負事にも利益があるといわれ、最近ではその勝負事が受験生のものとなっており、[[入学試験|受験シーズン]]には合格祈願の場所として訪れているが、最近では[[失われた20年|平成の長期的な不況]]で、様変わりして本来の目的であるいくつかの勝負事に回帰して[[就職活動]]中の学生・金運上昇を願う人の参拝も増えている。遠隔地在住や用事で訪問不可能な場合には祈祷受付を郵送で行っている<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html| title=お松大権現（阿南市）や王子神社（徳島市） 受験生ら続々参拝| publisher=徳島新聞| accessdate=2014-04-05| archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407095141/http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html| archivedate=2014-04-07| url-status=dead|url-status-date=2018-03}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /><ref name="村上2002">[[#村上2002|村上2002年]]、150-161頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/index.html|title=Home|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/kitou-kigan.html|title=祈祷|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>。また、境内には全国的にも珍しいネコの[[狛犬]]もある。
== 沿革 == * [[江戸時代]] - 開所する。 * [[昭和初期]] - この頃までに、祭神名が義理大権現からお松大権現に改称。 * 1963年 - 本殿が現在の場所に遷宮<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=お松大権現｜合格・必勝祈願の「猫神さん」 |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |website=nekogami.jp |access-date=2023-02-22}}</ref>。

== 境内社 == [[猫]]にまつわる置物や建物が建てられている。また、1万体以上の招き猫が奉納され、借りて持ち帰ることができる<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/>。 * 招き猫 * 祈願絵馬 * さすり猫 * 資料館 * ジャンボ猫

{{神社 |名称 = お松大権現 |画像 = [[File:Omatsugongen hall of worship.JPG|300px]] |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]字不ケ63番地 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|54|53.8|N|134|33|9.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神 =（儀）義理大権現（お松、[[仁木義広]]の孫、宅左衛門の女） |神体 =槇の木 |社格 = |創建 = [[江戸時代]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''お松大権現'''（おまつ-だいごんげん）は、[[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]にある[[神社]]である。宗教法人お松権現社が設置。通称「'''猫神さん'''<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8" />」と呼ばれており、日本三大怪猫伝の舞台の一つである。
== 歴史 == [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,1.JPG|thumb|250px|猫の狛犬]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,10.JPG|thumb|250px|境内に植えられている桜]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,13.JPG|thumb|250px|参拝道路]] [[File:Omatsu gongen Shrine office No,2.JPG|thumb|250px|招き猫・御神籤を販売している物販店]] [[江戸時代]]の前期の[[貞享]]年間、[[徳島藩]][[加茂村 (徳島県)|加茂村]]（現・阿南市[[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]）の庄屋が不作である村を救うために[[富豪]]に金を借り、すでに返済したにもかかわらず、富豪の策略で未返済の濡れ衣を着せられ、失意の内に病死した。そこで、[[借金]]の担保になっていた土地は富豪に取り上げられてしまう。庄屋の妻のお松は[[奉行所]]に訴え出るも、富豪に買収された奉行は不当な裁きを下す。お松がそれを不服として藩主に直訴した結果、直訴の罪により[[処刑]]され、お松の飼っていた[[三毛猫]]が[[化け猫]]となり、富豪や奉行らの家を滅ぼしたという伝説に由来する<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=御由緒 |publisher=お松大権現 |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead |url-status-date=2023-02-22}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html| title=お松大権現| publisher=阿南市観光協会 きらりあなん| accessdate=2014-04-05| archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407070625/http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html| archivedate=2014-04-07| url-status=dead|url-status-date=2018-03}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=お松大権現|publisher=徳島県観光情報サイト阿波ナビ | accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=お松大権現|publisher=阿南市 | accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=お松大権現｜合格・必勝祈願の「猫神さま」 |url=https://nekogami.jp/ |website=nekogami.jp |access-date=2023-02-22 |publisher=お松大権現 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230222093117/https://nekogami.jp/ |archive-date=2023-02-22}}</ref>。
お松の死因については、お松のみ処刑<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=阿波のがんこもん |date=2021年1月15日 |publisher=阿波の歴史を小説にする会 |series=阿波の歴史小説 ; 41 |id={{NDLBibID|031210439}} |page=190}}</ref>、お松と猫どちらも処刑<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=ふるさと加茂谷の昔がたり |volume=第1集 |editor=加茂谷郷土研究会 |page=37}}</ref>、自殺<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=阿南の伝説 |date=1976年7月1日 |publisher=小山助学館 |id={{NDLSearch|R100000136-I1130282269463906688}} |page=56}}</ref>をしたという伝承がある。
=== 勝負事に利益がある神社 === 直訴によって悪人を倒したという伝説から、勝負事にも利益があるといわれ、最近ではその勝負事が受験生のものとなっており、[[入学試験|受験シーズン]]には合格祈願の場所として訪れているが、最近では[[失われた20年|平成の長期的な不況]]で、様変わりして本来の目的であるいくつかの勝負事に回帰して[[就職活動]]中の学生・金運上昇を願う人の参拝も増えている。遠隔地在住や用事で訪問不可能な場合には祈祷受付を郵送で行っている<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html| title=お松大権現（阿南市）や王子神社（徳島市） 受験生ら続々参拝| publisher=徳島新聞| accessdate=2014-04-05| archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407095141/http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html| archivedate=2014-04-07| url-status=dead|url-status-date=2018-03}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /><ref name="村上2002">[[#村上2002|村上2002年]]、150-161頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/index.html|title=Home|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/kitou-kigan.html|title=祈祷|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>。また、境内には全国的にも珍しいネコの[[狛犬]]もある。
== 沿革 == * [[江戸時代]] - 開所する。 * [[昭和初期]] - この頃までに、祭神名が義理大権現からお松大権現に改称。 * 1963年 - 本殿が現在の場所に遷宮<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=お松大権現｜合格・必勝祈願の「猫神さん」 |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |website=nekogami.jp |access-date=2023-02-22}}</ref>。
== 祭事 == {| class="wikitable" !内容<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=御祭典|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!開催日<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |お正月祭 |[[1月1日]]から同月3日 |- |お松の命日 |[[旧暦]]の[[3月15日]] |- |御祭典 |[[5月1日]] |- |月並祭 |毎月1日と15日 |}
== 境内社 == [[猫]]にまつわる置物や建物が建てられている。また、1万体以上の招き猫が奉納され、借りて持ち帰ることができる<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/>。 * 招き猫 * 祈願絵馬 * さすり猫 * 資料館 * ジャンボ猫
<gallery> File:Omatsugongen Archives.JPG|招き猫 File:Omatsugongen prayer No,2.JPG|祈祷絵馬 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.914944|134.552722}}|caption=お松大権現|width=256}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=村上健司|title=日本全国妖怪スポット|year=2011|publisher=[[汐文社]]|volume=3|isbn=978-4-8113-8805-2|pages=124|ref={{SfnRef|村上|2011}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[阿南市]] * [[王子神社 (徳島市)]] - こちらの神社もお松とお松の寵愛した猫が祀ってある * [[化け猫#鍋島の化け猫騒動|鍋島の化け猫騒動]] * [[有馬頼義]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Omatsu Daigongen | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omatsugongen hall of worship.JPG|300px]] |location = [[Tokushima Prefecture]], [[Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], {{ill|Kamochō (Anan, Tokushima)|ja|加茂町 (阿南市)}} 63 Fukuke |coordinates = {{Coord|33|54|53.8|N|134|33|9.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Deities = (Honorary) Giri Daigongen (Omatsu, granddaughter of {{ill|Niki Yoshihiro|ja|仁木義広}}, daughter of Takusaimon) |SacredObject= Maki tree |ShrineRank = |Established = [[Edo period]] |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Festival = |Rituals = }}
'''Omatsu Daigongen''' (お松大権現) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kamochō (Anan, Tokushima)|Kamochō]], [[Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is managed by the religious corporation Omatsu Gongen-sha. Commonly referred to as the "Cat God Shrine" (猫神さん)<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8" />, it is one of the settings for Japan’s "Three Great Bakeneko Legends."
== History == [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,1.JPG|thumb|250px|Cat-shaped komainu]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,10.JPG|thumb|250px|Cherry blossoms in the shrine precincts]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,13.JPG|thumb|250px|Access road to the shrine]] [[File:Omatsu gongen Shrine office No,2.JPG|thumb|250px|Shop selling maneki-neko and omikuji]] During the early [[Edo period]], in the [[Jōkyō era]] (1684–1688), a village headman in {{ill|Kamomura (Tokushima)|ja|加茂村 (徳島県)}} (modern-day Kamochō, Anan City) borrowed money from a wealthy merchant to save the famine-stricken village. Although the debt was repaid, the merchant framed him for default. The headman, deeply distressed, fell ill and died. His land, which had been pledged as collateral, was seized by the merchant. The headman’s wife, Omatsu, petitioned the magistrate’s office but faced an unjust ruling due to the merchant’s bribery. When she appealed directly to the domain lord, Omatsu was executed for the act of direct petitioning. According to legend, her pet calico cat transformed into a [[bakeneko]] (monster cat) and destroyed the homes of the merchant and the magistrate.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=Omatsu Gongen Shrine History |publisher=Omatsu Daigongen |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Awa Navi Tokushima Tourism Info |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref>
The exact cause of Omatsu’s death varies among legends. Some say only Omatsu was executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Awa’s Stubborn People |publisher=Awa Historical Fiction Society |date=2021 |page=190}}</ref> others suggest both Omatsu and her cat were executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tales of Kamotani’s Past |editor=Kamotani Local History Research Association |page=37}}</ref> or that Omatsu committed suicide.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Legends of Anan |publisher=Koyama Bookstore |date=1976 |page=56}}</ref>
=== Shrine Associated with Victory === Due to the legend of Omatsu's direct petitioning and subsequent defeat of evil, the shrine is believed to bring success in competitions. In recent years, this belief has extended to students praying for success in exams, particularly during the [[university entrance examination]] season. Amid [[Japan's Lost Decades]], the shrine has seen increased visits from job seekers and those praying for financial success. The shrine also accepts mail-in prayer requests for those unable to visit in person.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen (Anan City) and Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City) Attract Students|publisher=Tokushima Shimbun|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /> Uniquely, the shrine features cat-shaped [[komainu]] (guardian statues).
== Timeline == * [[Edo period]] – Shrine founded. * Early [[Shōwa period]] – The enshrined deity's name was changed from Giri Daigongen to Omatsu Daigongen. * 1963 – Main shrine relocated to its current location.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omatsu Daigongen: Cat God for Success and Victory |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |accessdate=2023-02-22}}</ref>
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" !Event<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=Festivals|publisher=Omatsu Daigongen|accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!Date<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |New Year Festival |January 1–3 |- |Omatsu's Memorial Day |March 15 (lunar calendar) |- |Grand Festival |May 1 |- |Monthly Festival |1st and 15th of every month |}
== Shrine Precincts == The precincts are adorned with cat-related objects and buildings. Over 10,000 maneki-neko (beckoning cat statues) have been dedicated and can be borrowed and taken home.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/> Notable features include: * Maneki-neko * Prayer ema * Rubbing cat statues * Archives * Giant cat statue
<gallery> File:Omatsugongen Archives.JPG|Maneki-neko File:Omatsugongen prayer No,2.JPG|Prayer ema </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * [[Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]] * {{ill|Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)}} – Also enshrines Omatsu and her beloved cat. * {{ill|Bakeneko#Saga of the Nabeshima Bakeneko|鍋島の化け猫騒動|lt=Nabeshima Bakeneko Incident}} * {{ill|Yoriyoshi Arima|ja|有馬頼義}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Omatsugongen}} * [https://nekogami.jp/ Omatsu Daigongen Official Website]

<gallery> File:Omatsugongen Archives.JPG|Maneki-neko File:Omatsugongen prayer No,2.JPG|Prayer ema </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * [[Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]] * {{ill|Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)}} – Also enshrines Omatsu and her beloved cat. * {{ill|Bakeneko#Saga of the Nabeshima Bakeneko|鍋島の化け猫騒動|lt=Nabeshima Bakeneko Incident}} * {{ill|Yoriyoshi Arima|ja|有馬頼義}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Omatsu Daigongen | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omatsugongen hall of worship.JPG|300px]] |location = [[Tokushima Prefecture]], [[Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], {{ill|Kamochō (Anan, Tokushima)|ja|加茂町 (阿南市)}} 63 Fukuke |coordinates = {{Coord|33|54|53.8|N|134|33|9.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Deities = (Honorary) Giri Daigongen (Omatsu, granddaughter of {{ill|Niki Yoshihiro|ja|仁木義広}}, daughter of Takusaimon) |SacredObject= Maki tree |ShrineRank = |Established = [[Edo period]] |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Festival = |Rituals = }}
'''Omatsu Daigongen''' (お松大権現) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kamochō (Anan, Tokushima)|Kamochō]], [[Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is managed by the religious corporation Omatsu Gongen-sha. Commonly referred to as the "Cat God Shrine" (猫神さん)<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8" />, it is one of the settings for Japan’s "Three Great Bakeneko Legends."
== History == [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,1.JPG|thumb|250px|Cat-shaped komainu]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,10.JPG|thumb|250px|Cherry blossoms in the shrine precincts]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,13.JPG|thumb|250px|Access road to the shrine]] [[File:Omatsu gongen Shrine office No,2.JPG|thumb|250px|Shop selling maneki-neko and omikuji]] During the early [[Edo period]], in the [[Jōkyō era]] (1684–1688), a village headman in {{ill|Kamomura (Tokushima)|ja|加茂村 (徳島県)}} (modern-day Kamochō, Anan City) borrowed money from a wealthy merchant to save the famine-stricken village. Although the debt was repaid, the merchant framed him for default. The headman, deeply distressed, fell ill and died. His land, which had been pledged as collateral, was seized by the merchant. The headman’s wife, Omatsu, petitioned the magistrate’s office but faced an unjust ruling due to the merchant’s bribery. When she appealed directly to the domain lord, Omatsu was executed for the act of direct petitioning. According to legend, her pet calico cat transformed into a [[bakeneko]] (monster cat) and destroyed the homes of the merchant and the magistrate.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=Omatsu Gongen Shrine History |publisher=Omatsu Daigongen |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Awa Navi Tokushima Tourism Info |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref>
The exact cause of Omatsu’s death varies among legends. Some say only Omatsu was executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Awa’s Stubborn People |publisher=Awa Historical Fiction Society |date=2021 |page=190}}</ref> others suggest both Omatsu and her cat were executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tales of Kamotani’s Past |editor=Kamotani Local History Research Association |page=37}}</ref> or that Omatsu committed suicide.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Legends of Anan |publisher=Koyama Bookstore |date=1976 |page=56}}</ref>
=== Shrine Associated with Victory === Due to the legend of Omatsu's direct petitioning and subsequent defeat of evil, the shrine is believed to bring success in competitions. In recent years, this belief has extended to students praying for success in exams, particularly during the [[university entrance examination]] season. Amid [[Japan's Lost Decades]], the shrine has seen increased visits from job seekers and those praying for financial success. The shrine also accepts mail-in prayer requests for those unable to visit in person.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen (Anan City) and Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City) Attract Students|publisher=Tokushima Shimbun|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /> Uniquely, the shrine features cat-shaped [[komainu]] (guardian statues).
== Timeline == * [[Edo period]] – Shrine founded. * Early [[Shōwa period]] – The enshrined deity's name was changed from Giri Daigongen to Omatsu Daigongen. * 1963 – Main shrine relocated to its current location.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omatsu Daigongen: Cat God for Success and Victory |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |accessdate=2023-02-22}}</ref>
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" !Event<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=Festivals|publisher=Omatsu Daigongen|accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!Date<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |New Year Festival |January 1–3 |- |Omatsu's Memorial Day |March 15 (lunar calendar) |- |Grand Festival |May 1 |- |Monthly Festival |1st and 15th of every month |}
== Shrine Precincts == The precincts are adorned with cat-related objects and buildings. Over 10,000 maneki-neko (beckoning cat statues) have been dedicated and can be borrowed and taken home.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/> Notable features include: * Maneki-neko * Prayer ema * Rubbing cat statues * Archives * Giant cat statue
<gallery> File:Omatsugongen Archives.JPG|Maneki-neko File:Omatsugongen prayer No,2.JPG|Prayer ema </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * [[Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]] * {{ill|Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)}} – Also enshrines Omatsu and her beloved cat. * {{ill|Bakeneko#Saga of the Nabeshima Bakeneko|鍋島の化け猫騒動|lt=Nabeshima Bakeneko Incident}} * {{ill|Yoriyoshi Arima|ja|有馬頼義}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Omatsugongen}} * [https://nekogami.jp/ Omatsu Daigongen Official Website]

The shrine also accepts mail-in prayer requests for those unable to visit in person.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen (Anan City) and Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City) Attract Students|publisher=Tokushima Shimbun|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /> Uniquely, the shrine features cat-shaped [[komainu]] (guardian statues).

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = お松大権現 |画像 = [[File:Omatsugongen hall of worship.JPG|300px]] |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]字不ケ63番地 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|54|53.8|N|134|33|9.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神 =（儀）義理大権現（お松、[[仁木義広]]の孫、宅左衛門の女） |神体 =槇の木 |社格 = |創建 = [[江戸時代]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''お松大権現'''（おまつ-だいごんげん）は、[[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]にある[[神社]]である。宗教法人お松権現社が設置。通称「'''猫神さん'''<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8" />」と呼ばれており、日本三大怪猫伝の舞台の一つである。
== 歴史 == [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,1.JPG|thumb|250px|猫の狛犬]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,10.JPG|thumb|250px|境内に植えられている桜]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,13.JPG|thumb|250px|参拝道路]] [[File:Omatsu gongen Shrine office No,2.JPG|thumb|250px|招き猫・御神籤を販売している物販店]] [[江戸時代]]の前期の[[貞享]]年間、[[徳島藩]][[加茂村 (徳島県)|加茂村]]（現・阿南市[[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]）の庄屋が不作である村を救うために[[富豪]]に金を借り、すでに返済したにもかかわらず、富豪の策略で未返済の濡れ衣を着せられ、失意の内に病死した。そこで、[[借金]]の担保になっていた土地は富豪に取り上げられてしまう。庄屋の妻のお松は[[奉行所]]に訴え出るも、富豪に買収された奉行は不当な裁きを下す。お松がそれを不服として藩主に直訴した結果、直訴の罪により[[処刑]]され、お松の飼っていた[[三毛猫]]が[[化け猫]]となり、富豪や奉行らの家を滅ぼしたという伝説に由来する<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=御由緒 |publisher=お松大権現 |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead |url-status-date=2023-02-22}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html| title=お松大権現| publisher=阿南市観光協会 きらりあなん| accessdate=2014-04-05| archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407070625/http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html| archivedate=2014-04-07| url-status=dead|url-status-date=2018-03}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=お松大権現|publisher=徳島県観光情報サイト阿波ナビ | accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=お松大権現|publisher=阿南市 | accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=お松大権現｜合格・必勝祈願の「猫神さま」 |url=https://nekogami.jp/ |website=nekogami.jp |access-date=2023-02-22 |publisher=お松大権現 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230222093117/https://nekogami.jp/ |archive-date=2023-02-22}}</ref>。
お松の死因については、お松のみ処刑<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=阿波のがんこもん |date=2021年1月15日 |publisher=阿波の歴史を小説にする会 |series=阿波の歴史小説 ; 41 |id={{NDLBibID|031210439}} |page=190}}</ref>、お松と猫どちらも処刑<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=ふるさと加茂谷の昔がたり |volume=第1集 |editor=加茂谷郷土研究会 |page=37}}</ref>、自殺<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=阿南の伝説 |date=1976年7月1日 |publisher=小山助学館 |id={{NDLSearch|R100000136-I1130282269463906688}} |page=56}}</ref>をしたという伝承がある。
=== 勝負事に利益がある神社 === 直訴によって悪人を倒したという伝説から、勝負事にも利益があるといわれ、最近ではその勝負事が受験生のものとなっており、[[入学試験|受験シーズン]]には合格祈願の場所として訪れているが、最近では[[失われた20年|平成の長期的な不況]]で、様変わりして本来の目的であるいくつかの勝負事に回帰して[[就職活動]]中の学生・金運上昇を願う人の参拝も増えている。遠隔地在住や用事で訪問不可能な場合には祈祷受付を郵送で行っている<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html| title=お松大権現（阿南市）や王子神社（徳島市） 受験生ら続々参拝| publisher=徳島新聞| accessdate=2014-04-05| archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407095141/http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html| archivedate=2014-04-07| url-status=dead|url-status-date=2018-03}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /><ref name="村上2002">[[#村上2002|村上2002年]]、150-161頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/index.html|title=Home|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/kitou-kigan.html|title=祈祷|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>。また、境内には全国的にも珍しいネコの[[狛犬]]もある。
== 沿革 == * [[江戸時代]] - 開所する。 * [[昭和初期]] - この頃までに、祭神名が義理大権現からお松大権現に改称。 * 1963年 - 本殿が現在の場所に遷宮<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=お松大権現｜合格・必勝祈願の「猫神さん」 |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |website=nekogami.jp |access-date=2023-02-22}}</ref>。
== 祭事 == {| class="wikitable" !内容<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=御祭典|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!開催日<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |お正月祭 |[[1月1日]]から同月3日 |- |お松の命日 |[[旧暦]]の[[3月15日]] |- |御祭典 |[[5月1日]] |- |月並祭 |毎月1日と15日 |}
== 境内社 == [[猫]]にまつわる置物や建物が建てられている。また、1万体以上の招き猫が奉納され、借りて持ち帰ることができる<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/>。 * 招き猫 * 祈願絵馬 * さすり猫 * 資料館 * ジャンボ猫
<gallery> File:Omatsugongen Archives.JPG|招き猫 File:Omatsugongen prayer No,2.JPG|祈祷絵馬 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.914944|134.552722}}|caption=お松大権現|width=256}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=村上健司|title=日本全国妖怪スポット|year=2011|publisher=[[汐文社]]|volume=3|isbn=978-4-8113-8805-2|pages=124|ref={{SfnRef|村上|2011}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[阿南市]] * [[王子神社 (徳島市)]] - こちらの神社もお松とお松の寵愛した猫が祀ってある * [[化け猫#鍋島の化け猫騒動|鍋島の化け猫騒動]] * [[有馬頼義]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[お松大権現]] to [[Omatsu Daigongen]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = お松大権現 |画像 = [[File:Omatsugongen hall of worship.JPG|300px]] |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]字不ケ63番地 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|54|53.8|N|134|33|9.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神 =（儀）義理大権現（お松、[[仁木義広]]の孫、宅左衛門の女） |神体 =槇の木 |社格 = |創建 = [[江戸時代]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''お松大権現'''（おまつ-だいごんげん）は、[[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]にある[[神社]]である。宗教法人お松権現社が設置。通称「'''猫神さん'''<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8" />」と呼ばれており、日本三大怪猫伝の舞台の一つである。
== 歴史 == [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,1.JPG|thumb|250px|猫の狛犬]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,10.JPG|thumb|250px|境内に植えられている桜]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,13.JPG|thumb|250px|参拝道路]] [[File:Omatsu gongen Shrine office No,2.JPG|thumb|250px|招き猫・御神籤を販売している物販店]] [[江戸時代]]の前期の[[貞享]]年間、[[徳島藩]][[加茂村 (徳島県)|加茂村]]（現・阿南市[[加茂町 (阿南市)|加茂町]]）の庄屋が不作である村を救うために[[富豪]]に金を借り、すでに返済したにもかかわらず、富豪の策略で未返済の濡れ衣を着せられ、失意の内に病死した。そこで、[[借金]]の担保になっていた土地は富豪に取り上げられてしまう。庄屋の妻のお松は[[奉行所]]に訴え出るも、富豪に買収された奉行は不当な裁きを下す。お松がそれを不服として藩主に直訴した結果、直訴の罪により[[処刑]]され、お松の飼っていた[[三毛猫]]が[[化け猫]]となり、富豪や奉行らの家を滅ぼしたという伝説に由来する<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=御由緒 |publisher=お松大権現 |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead |url-status-date=2023-02-22}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html| title=お松大権現| publisher=阿南市観光協会 きらりあなん| accessdate=2014-04-05| archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407070625/http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html| archivedate=2014-04-07| url-status=dead|url-status-date=2018-03}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=お松大権現|publisher=徳島県観光情報サイト阿波ナビ | accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=お松大権現|publisher=阿南市 | accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=お松大権現｜合格・必勝祈願の「猫神さま」 |url=https://nekogami.jp/ |website=nekogami.jp |access-date=2023-02-22 |publisher=お松大権現 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230222093117/https://nekogami.jp/ |archive-date=2023-02-22}}</ref>。
お松の死因については、お松のみ処刑<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=阿波のがんこもん |date=2021年1月15日 |publisher=阿波の歴史を小説にする会 |series=阿波の歴史小説 ; 41 |id={{NDLBibID|031210439}} |page=190}}</ref>、お松と猫どちらも処刑<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=ふるさと加茂谷の昔がたり |volume=第1集 |editor=加茂谷郷土研究会 |page=37}}</ref>、自殺<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=阿南の伝説 |date=1976年7月1日 |publisher=小山助学館 |id={{NDLSearch|R100000136-I1130282269463906688}} |page=56}}</ref>をしたという伝承がある。
=== 勝負事に利益がある神社 === 直訴によって悪人を倒したという伝説から、勝負事にも利益があるといわれ、最近ではその勝負事が受験生のものとなっており、[[入学試験|受験シーズン]]には合格祈願の場所として訪れているが、最近では[[失われた20年|平成の長期的な不況]]で、様変わりして本来の目的であるいくつかの勝負事に回帰して[[就職活動]]中の学生・金運上昇を願う人の参拝も増えている。遠隔地在住や用事で訪問不可能な場合には祈祷受付を郵送で行っている<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html| title=お松大権現（阿南市）や王子神社（徳島市） 受験生ら続々参拝| publisher=徳島新聞| accessdate=2014-04-05| archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407095141/http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html| archivedate=2014-04-07| url-status=dead|url-status-date=2018-03}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /><ref name="村上2002">[[#村上2002|村上2002年]]、150-161頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/index.html|title=Home|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/kitou-kigan.html|title=祈祷|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>。また、境内には全国的にも珍しいネコの[[狛犬]]もある。
== 沿革 == * [[江戸時代]] - 開所する。 * [[昭和初期]] - この頃までに、祭神名が義理大権現からお松大権現に改称。 * 1963年 - 本殿が現在の場所に遷宮<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=お松大権現｜合格・必勝祈願の「猫神さん」 |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |website=nekogami.jp |access-date=2023-02-22}}</ref>。
== 祭事 == {| class="wikitable" !内容<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|和書| url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=御祭典|publisher=お松大権現 | accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!開催日<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |お正月祭 |[[1月1日]]から同月3日 |- |お松の命日 |[[旧暦]]の[[3月15日]] |- |御祭典 |[[5月1日]] |- |月並祭 |毎月1日と15日 |}
== 境内社 == [[猫]]にまつわる置物や建物が建てられている。また、1万体以上の招き猫が奉納され、借りて持ち帰ることができる<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/>。 * 招き猫 * 祈願絵馬 * さすり猫 * 資料館 * ジャンボ猫
<gallery> File:Omatsugongen Archives.JPG|招き猫 File:Omatsugongen prayer No,2.JPG|祈祷絵馬 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.914944|134.552722}}|caption=お松大権現|width=256}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=村上健司|title=日本全国妖怪スポット|year=2011|publisher=[[汐文社]]|volume=3|isbn=978-4-8113-8805-2|pages=124|ref={{SfnRef|村上|2011}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[阿南市]] * [[王子神社 (徳島市)]] - こちらの神社もお松とお松の寵愛した猫が祀ってある * [[化け猫#鍋島の化け猫騒動|鍋島の化け猫騒動]] * [[有馬頼義]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お松大権現]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17225698]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Omatsu Daigongen | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Omatsugongen hall of worship.JPG |location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], {{ill|Kamochō (Anan, Tokushima)|ja|加茂町 (阿南市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō (Anan, Tokushima)|qq=|1=Kamochō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398872}} 63 Fukuke |coordinates = {{Coord|33.914944|N|134.552722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = (Honorary) Giri Daigongen (Omatsu, granddaughter of {{ill|Niki Yoshihiro|ja|仁木義広|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niki Yoshihiro|qq=|1=Nikki Yoshihiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24872537}}, daughter of Takusaimon) |SacredObject= Maki tree |ShrineRank = |Established = [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Festival = |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Anan|qid=Q485583}} }}
'''Omatsu Daigongen''' (お松大権現) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kamochō (Anan, Tokushima)|Kamochō]], [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is managed by the religious corporation Omatsu Gongen-sha. Commonly referred to as the "Cat God Shrine" (猫神さん)<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8" />, it is one of the settings for Japan’s "Three Great Bakeneko Legends."
== History == [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,1.JPG|thumb|250px|Cat-shaped komainu]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,10.JPG|thumb|250px|Cherry blossoms in the shrine precincts]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,13.JPG|thumb|250px|Access road to the shrine]] [[File:Omatsu gongen Shrine office No,2.JPG|thumb|250px|Shop selling maneki-neko and omikuji]] During the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in the [[Jōkyō era]] (1684–1688), a village headman in {{ill|Kamomura (Tokushima)|ja|加茂村 (徳島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamomura (Tokushima)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day Kamochō, Anan City) borrowed money from a wealthy merchant to save the famine-stricken village. Although the debt was repaid, the merchant framed him for default. The headman, deeply distressed, fell ill and died. His land, which had been pledged as collateral, was seized by the merchant. The headman’s wife, Omatsu, petitioned the magistrate’s office but faced an unjust ruling due to the merchant’s bribery. When she appealed directly to the domain lord, Omatsu was executed for the act of direct petitioning. According to legend, her pet calico cat transformed into a [[:en:Bakeneko|Bakeneko]] (monster cat) and destroyed the homes of the merchant and the magistrate.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=Omatsu Gongen Shrine History |publisher=Omatsu Daigongen |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Awa Navi Tokushima Tourism Info |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref>
The exact cause of Omatsu’s death varies among legends. Some say only Omatsu was executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Awa’s Stubborn People |publisher=Awa Historical Fiction Society |date=2021 |page=190}}</ref> others suggest both Omatsu and her cat were executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tales of Kamotani’s Past |editor=Kamotani Local History Research Association |page=37}}</ref> or that Omatsu committed suicide.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Legends of Anan |publisher=Koyama Bookstore |date=1976 |page=56}}</ref>
=== Shrine Associated with Victory === Due to the legend of Omatsu's direct petitioning and subsequent defeat of evil, the shrine is believed to bring success in competitions. In recent years, this belief has extended to students praying for success in exams, particularly during the [[university entrance examination]] season. Amid [[Japan's Lost Decades]], the shrine has seen increased visits from job seekers and those praying for financial success. The shrine also accepts mail-in prayer requests for those unable to visit in person.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen (Anan City) and Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City) Attract Students|publisher=Tokushima Shimbun|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /> Uniquely, the shrine features cat-shaped [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (guardian statues).
== Timeline == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] – Shrine founded. * Early [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] – The enshrined deity's name was changed from Giri Daigongen to Omatsu Daigongen. * 1963 – Main shrine relocated to its current location.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omatsu Daigongen: Cat God for Success and Victory |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |accessdate=2023-02-22}}</ref>
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" !Event<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=Festivals|publisher=Omatsu Daigongen|accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!Date<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |New Year Festival |January 1–3 |- |Omatsu's Memorial Day |March 15 (lunar calendar) |- |Grand Festival |May 1 |- |Monthly Festival |1st and 15th of every month |}
== Shrine Precincts == The precincts are adorned with cat-related objects and buildings. Over 10,000 maneki-neko (beckoning cat statues) have been dedicated and can be borrowed and taken home.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/> Notable features include: * Maneki-neko * Prayer ema * Rubbing cat statues * Archives * Giant cat statue
<gallery> File:Omatsugongen Archives.JPG|Maneki-neko File:Omatsugongen prayer No,2.JPG|Prayer ema </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]] * {{ill|Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – Also enshrines Omatsu and her beloved cat. * {{ill|Bakeneko#Saga of the Nabeshima Bakeneko|鍋島の化け猫騒動|lt=Nabeshima Bakeneko Incident|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakeneko#Saga of the Nabeshima Bakeneko|qq=}} * {{ill|Yoriyoshi Arima|ja|有馬頼義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoriyoshi Arima|qq=|1=Yorichika Arima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516850}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Omatsugongen}} * [https://nekogami.jp/ Omatsu Daigongen Official Website]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お松大権現]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17225698]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Omatsu Daigongen | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Omatsugongen hall of worship.JPG |location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], {{ill|Kamochō (Anan, Tokushima)|ja|加茂町 (阿南市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō (Anan, Tokushima)|qq=|1=Kamochō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398872}} 63 Fukuke |coordinates = {{Coord|33.914944|N|134.552722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = (Honorary) Giri Daigongen (Omatsu, granddaughter of {{ill|Niki Yoshihiro|ja|仁木義広|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niki Yoshihiro|qq=|1=Nikki Yoshihiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24872537}}, daughter of Takusaimon) |SacredObject= Maki tree |ShrineRank = |Established = [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Festival = |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Anan|WD=Q485583}} }}
'''Omatsu Daigongen''' (お松大権現) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kamochō (Anan, Tokushima)|Kamochō]], [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is managed by the religious corporation Omatsu Gongen-sha. Commonly referred to as the "Cat God Shrine" (猫神さん)<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8" />, it is one of the settings for Japan’s "Three Great Bakeneko Legends."

Although the debt was repaid, the merchant framed him for default. The headman, deeply distressed, fell ill and died. His land, which had been pledged as collateral, was seized by the merchant. The headman’s wife, Omatsu, petitioned the magistrate’s office but faced an unjust ruling due to the merchant’s bribery. When she appealed directly to the domain lord, Omatsu was executed for the act of direct petitioning. According to legend, her pet calico cat transformed into a [[:en:Bakeneko|Bakeneko]] (monster cat) and destroyed the homes of the merchant and the magistrate.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=Omatsu Gongen Shrine History |publisher=Omatsu Daigongen |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Awa Navi Tokushima Tourism Info |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref>
The exact cause of Omatsu’s death varies among legends. Some say only Omatsu was executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Awa’s Stubborn People |publisher=Awa Historical Fiction Society |date=2021 |page=190}}</ref> others suggest both Omatsu and her cat were executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tales of Kamotani’s Past |editor=Kamotani Local History Research Association |page=37}}</ref> or that Omatsu committed suicide.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Legends of Anan |publisher=Koyama Bookstore |date=1976 |page=56}}</ref>
=== Shrine Associated with Victory === Due to the legend of Omatsu's direct petitioning and subsequent defeat of evil, the shrine is believed to bring success in competitions. In recent years, this belief has extended to students praying for success in exams, particularly during the [[university entrance examination]] season. Amid [[Japan's Lost Decades]], the shrine has seen increased visits from job seekers and those praying for financial success. The shrine also accepts mail-in prayer requests for those unable to visit in person.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen (Anan City) and Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City) Attract Students|publisher=Tokushima Shimbun|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /> Uniquely, the shrine features cat-shaped [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (guardian statues).
== Timeline == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] – Shrine founded. * Early [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] – The enshrined deity's name was changed from Giri Daigongen to Omatsu Daigongen. * 1963 – Main shrine relocated to its current location.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omatsu Daigongen: Cat God for Success and Victory |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |accessdate=2023-02-22}}</ref>
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" !Event<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=Festivals|publisher=Omatsu Daigongen|accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!Date<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |New Year Festival |January 1–3 |- |Omatsu's Memorial Day |March 15 (lunar calendar) |- |Grand Festival |May 1 |- |Monthly Festival |1st and 15th of every month |}
== Shrine Precincts == The precincts are adorned with cat-related objects and buildings. Over 10,000 maneki-neko (beckoning cat statues) have been dedicated and can be borrowed and taken home.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/> Notable features include: * Maneki-neko * Prayer ema * Rubbing cat statues * Archives * Giant cat statue
<gallery> File:Omatsugongen Archives.JPG|Maneki-neko File:Omatsugongen prayer No,2.JPG|Prayer ema </gallery>

'''Omatsu Daigongen''' (お松大権現) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kamochō (Anan, Tokushima)|Kamochō]], [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is managed by the religious corporation Omatsu Gongen-sha. Commonly referred to as the "Cat God Shrine" (猫神さん)<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8" />, it is one of the settings for Japan’s "Three Great Bakeneko Legends."
== History == [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,1.JPG|thumb|250px|Cat-shaped komainu]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,10.JPG|thumb|250px|Cherry blossoms in the shrine precincts]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,13.JPG|thumb|250px|Access road to the shrine]] [[File:Omatsu gongen Shrine office No,2.JPG|thumb|250px|Shop selling maneki-neko and omikuji]] During the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in the [[Jōkyō era]] (1684–1688), a village headman in {{ill|Kamomura (Tokushima)|ja|加茂村 (徳島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamomura (Tokushima)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day Kamochō, Anan City) borrowed money from a wealthy merchant to save the famine-stricken village. Although the debt was repaid, the merchant framed him for default. The headman, deeply distressed, fell ill and died. His land, which had been pledged as collateral, was seized by the merchant. The headman’s wife, Omatsu, petitioned the magistrate’s office but faced an unjust ruling due to the merchant’s bribery. When she appealed directly to the domain lord, Omatsu was executed for the act of direct petitioning. According to legend, her pet calico cat transformed into a [[:en:Bakeneko|Bakeneko]] (monster cat) and destroyed the homes of the merchant and the magistrate.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=Omatsu Gongen Shrine History |publisher=Omatsu Daigongen |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Awa Navi Tokushima Tourism Info |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref>
The exact cause of Omatsu’s death varies among legends. Some say only Omatsu was executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Awa’s Stubborn People |publisher=Awa Historical Fiction Society |date=2021 |page=190}}</ref> others suggest both Omatsu and her cat were executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tales of Kamotani’s Past |editor=Kamotani Local History Research Association |page=37}}</ref> or that Omatsu committed suicide.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Legends of Anan |publisher=Koyama Bookstore |date=1976 |page=56}}</ref>
=== Shrine Associated with Victory === Due to the legend of Omatsu's direct petitioning and subsequent defeat of evil, the shrine is believed to bring success in competitions. In recent years, this belief has extended to students praying for success in exams, particularly during the [[university entrance examination]] season. Amid [[Japan's Lost Decades]], the shrine has seen increased visits from job seekers and those praying for financial success. The shrine also accepts mail-in prayer requests for those unable to visit in person.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen (Anan City) and Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City) Attract Students|publisher=Tokushima Shimbun|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /> Uniquely, the shrine features cat-shaped [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (guardian statues).
== Timeline == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] – Shrine founded. * Early [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] – The enshrined deity's name was changed from Giri Daigongen to Omatsu Daigongen. * 1963 – Main shrine relocated to its current location.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omatsu Daigongen: Cat God for Success and Victory |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |accessdate=2023-02-22}}</ref>
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" !Event<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=Festivals|publisher=Omatsu Daigongen|accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!Date<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |New Year Festival |January 1–3 |- |Omatsu's Memorial Day |March 15 (lunar calendar) |- |Grand Festival |May 1 |- |Monthly Festival |1st and 15th of every month |}
== Shrine Precincts == The precincts are adorned with cat-related objects and buildings. Over 10,000 maneki-neko (beckoning cat statues) have been dedicated and can be borrowed and taken home.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/> Notable features include: * Maneki-neko * Prayer ema * Rubbing cat statues * Archives * Giant cat statue
<gallery> File:Omatsugongen Archives.JPG|Maneki-neko File:Omatsugongen prayer No,2.JPG|Prayer ema </gallery>

The headman’s wife, Omatsu, petitioned the magistrate’s office but faced an unjust ruling due to the merchant’s bribery. When she appealed directly to the domain lord, Omatsu was executed for the act of direct petitioning. According to legend, her pet calico cat transformed into a [[:en:Bakeneko|Bakeneko]] (monster cat) and destroyed the homes of the merchant and the magistrate.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=Omatsu Gongen Shrine History |publisher=Omatsu Daigongen |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Awa Navi Tokushima Tourism Info |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref>
The exact cause of Omatsu’s death varies among legends. Some say only Omatsu was executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Awa’s Stubborn People |publisher=Awa Historical Fiction Society |date=2021 |page=190}}</ref> others suggest both Omatsu and her cat were executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tales of Kamotani’s Past |editor=Kamotani Local History Research Association |page=37}}</ref> or that Omatsu committed suicide.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Legends of Anan |publisher=Koyama Bookstore |date=1976 |page=56}}</ref>
=== Shrine Associated with Victory === Due to the legend of Omatsu's direct petitioning and subsequent defeat of evil, the shrine is believed to bring success in competitions. In recent years, this belief has extended to students praying for success in exams, particularly during the [[university entrance examination]] season. Amid [[Japan's Lost Decades]], the shrine has seen increased visits from job seekers and those praying for financial success. The shrine also accepts mail-in prayer requests for those unable to visit in person.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen (Anan City) and Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City) Attract Students|publisher=Tokushima Shimbun|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /> Uniquely, the shrine features cat-shaped [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (guardian statues).
== Timeline == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] – Shrine founded. * Early [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] – The enshrined deity's name was changed from Giri Daigongen to Omatsu Daigongen. * 1963 – Main shrine relocated to its current location.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omatsu Daigongen: Cat God for Success and Victory |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |accessdate=2023-02-22}}</ref>
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" !Event<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=Festivals|publisher=Omatsu Daigongen|accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!Date<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |New Year Festival |January 1–3 |- |Omatsu's Memorial Day |March 15 (lunar calendar) |- |Grand Festival |May 1 |- |Monthly Festival |1st and 15th of every month |}
== Shrine Precincts == The precincts are adorned with cat-related objects and buildings. Over 10,000 maneki-neko (beckoning cat statues) have been dedicated and can be borrowed and taken home.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/> Notable features include: * Maneki-neko * Prayer ema * Rubbing cat statues * Archives * Giant cat statue
<gallery> File:Omatsugongen Archives.JPG|Maneki-neko File:Omatsugongen prayer No,2.JPG|Prayer ema </gallery>

'''Omatsu Daigongen''' (お松大権現) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kamochō (Anan, Tokushima)|Kamochō]], [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is managed by the religious corporation Omatsu Gongen-sha. Commonly referred to as the "Cat God Shrine" (猫神さん)<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8" />, it is one of the settings for Japan’s "Three Great Bakeneko Legends."
== History == [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,1.JPG|thumb|250px|Cat-shaped komainu]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,10.JPG|thumb|250px|Cherry blossoms in the shrine precincts]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,13.JPG|thumb|250px|Access road to the shrine]] [[File:Omatsu gongen Shrine office No,2.JPG|thumb|250px|Shop selling maneki-neko and omikuji]] During the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in the [[Jōkyō era]] (1684–1688), a village headman in {{ill|Kamomura (Tokushima)|ja|加茂村 (徳島県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamomura (Tokushima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day Kamochō, Anan City) borrowed money from a wealthy merchant to save the famine-stricken village. Although the debt was repaid, the merchant framed him for default. The headman, deeply distressed, fell ill and died. His land, which had been pledged as collateral, was seized by the merchant. The headman’s wife, Omatsu, petitioned the magistrate’s office but faced an unjust ruling due to the merchant’s bribery. When she appealed directly to the domain lord, Omatsu was executed for the act of direct petitioning. According to legend, her pet calico cat transformed into a [[:en:Bakeneko|Bakeneko]] (monster cat) and destroyed the homes of the merchant and the magistrate.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=Omatsu Gongen Shrine History |publisher=Omatsu Daigongen |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Awa Navi Tokushima Tourism Info |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref>
The exact cause of Omatsu’s death varies among legends. Some say only Omatsu was executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Awa’s Stubborn People |publisher=Awa Historical Fiction Society |date=2021 |page=190}}</ref> others suggest both Omatsu and her cat were executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tales of Kamotani’s Past |editor=Kamotani Local History Research Association |page=37}}</ref> or that Omatsu committed suicide.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Legends of Anan |publisher=Koyama Bookstore |date=1976 |page=56}}</ref>
=== Shrine Associated with Victory === Due to the legend of Omatsu's direct petitioning and subsequent defeat of evil, the shrine is believed to bring success in competitions. In recent years, this belief has extended to students praying for success in exams, particularly during the [[university entrance examination]] season. Amid [[Japan's Lost Decades]], the shrine has seen increased visits from job seekers and those praying for financial success. The shrine also accepts mail-in prayer requests for those unable to visit in person.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen (Anan City) and Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City) Attract Students|publisher=Tokushima Shimbun|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /> Uniquely, the shrine features cat-shaped [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (guardian statues).
== Timeline == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] – Shrine founded. * Early [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] – The enshrined deity's name was changed from Giri Daigongen to Omatsu Daigongen. * 1963 – Main shrine relocated to its current location.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omatsu Daigongen: Cat God for Success and Victory |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |accessdate=2023-02-22}}</ref>
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" !Event<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=Festivals|publisher=Omatsu Daigongen|accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!Date<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |New Year Festival |January 1–3 |- |Omatsu's Memorial Day |March 15 (lunar calendar) |- |Grand Festival |May 1 |- |Monthly Festival |1st and 15th of every month |}
== Shrine Precincts == The precincts are adorned with cat-related objects and buildings. Over 10,000 maneki-neko (beckoning cat statues) have been dedicated and can be borrowed and taken home.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/> Notable features include: * Maneki-neko * Prayer ema * Rubbing cat statues * Archives * Giant cat statue

The headman’s wife, Omatsu, petitioned the magistrate’s office but faced an unjust ruling due to the merchant’s bribery. When she appealed directly to the domain lord, Omatsu was executed for the act of direct petitioning. According to legend, her pet calico cat transformed into a [[:en:Bakeneko|Bakeneko]] (monster cat) and destroyed the homes of the merchant and the magistrate.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=Omatsu Gongen Shrine History |publisher=Omatsu Daigongen |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Awa Navi Tokushima Tourism Info |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref>
The exact cause of Omatsu’s death varies among legends. Some say only Omatsu was executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Awa’s Stubborn People |publisher=Awa Historical Fiction Society |date=2021 |page=190}}</ref> others suggest both Omatsu and her cat were executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tales of Kamotani’s Past |editor=Kamotani Local History Research Association |page=37}}</ref> or that Omatsu committed suicide.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Legends of Anan |publisher=Koyama Bookstore |date=1976 |page=56}}</ref>
=== Shrine Associated with Victory === Due to the legend of Omatsu's direct petitioning and subsequent defeat of evil, the shrine is believed to bring success in competitions. In recent years, this belief has extended to students praying for success in exams, particularly during the [[university entrance examination]] season. Amid [[Japan's Lost Decades]], the shrine has seen increased visits from job seekers and those praying for financial success. The shrine also accepts mail-in prayer requests for those unable to visit in person.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen (Anan City) and Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City) Attract Students|publisher=Tokushima Shimbun|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /> Uniquely, the shrine features cat-shaped [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (guardian statues).
== Timeline == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] – Shrine founded. * Early [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] – The enshrined deity's name was changed from Giri Daigongen to Omatsu Daigongen. * 1963 – Main shrine relocated to its current location.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omatsu Daigongen: Cat God for Success and Victory |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |accessdate=2023-02-22}}</ref>
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" !Event<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=Festivals|publisher=Omatsu Daigongen|accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!Date<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |New Year Festival |January 1–3 |- |Omatsu's Memorial Day |March 15 (lunar calendar) |- |Grand Festival |May 1 |- |Monthly Festival |1st and 15th of every month |}
== Shrine Precincts == The precincts are adorned with cat-related objects and buildings. Over 10,000 maneki-neko (beckoning cat statues) have been dedicated and can be borrowed and taken home.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/> Notable features include: * Maneki-neko * Prayer ema * Rubbing cat statues * Archives * Giant cat statue
<gallery> File:Omatsugongen Archives.JPG|Maneki-neko File:Omatsugongen prayer No,2.JPG|Prayer ema </gallery>

'''Omatsu Daigongen''' (お松大権現) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kamochō (Anan, Tokushima)|Kamochō]], [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is managed by the religious corporation Omatsu Gongen-sha. Commonly referred to as the "Cat God Shrine" (猫神さん)<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8" />, it is one of the settings for Japan’s "Three Great Bakeneko Legends."
== History == [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,1.JPG|thumb|250px|Cat-shaped komainu]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,10.JPG|thumb|250px|Cherry blossoms in the shrine precincts]] [[File:Omatsugongen in 2014-4-4 No,13.JPG|thumb|250px|Access road to the shrine]] [[File:Omatsu gongen Shrine office No,2.JPG|thumb|250px|Shop selling maneki-neko and omikuji]] During the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in the [[Jōkyō era]] (1684–1688), a village headman in {{ill|Kamomura (Tokushima)|ja|加茂村 (徳島県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamomura (Tokushima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day Kamochō, Anan City) borrowed money from a wealthy merchant to save the famine-stricken village. Although the debt was repaid, the merchant framed him for default. The headman, deeply distressed, fell ill and died. His land, which had been pledged as collateral, was seized by the merchant. The headman’s wife, Omatsu, petitioned the magistrate’s office but faced an unjust ruling due to the merchant’s bribery. When she appealed directly to the domain lord, Omatsu was executed for the act of direct petitioning. According to legend, her pet calico cat transformed into a [[:en:Bakeneko|Bakeneko]] (monster cat) and destroyed the homes of the merchant and the magistrate.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=Omatsu Gongen Shrine History |publisher=Omatsu Daigongen |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Awa Navi Tokushima Tourism Info |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref>
The exact cause of Omatsu’s death varies among legends. Some say only Omatsu was executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Awa’s Stubborn People |publisher=Awa Historical Fiction Society |date=2021 |page=190}}</ref> others suggest both Omatsu and her cat were executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tales of Kamotani’s Past |editor=Kamotani Local History Research Association |page=37}}</ref> or that Omatsu committed suicide.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Legends of Anan |publisher=Koyama Bookstore |date=1976 |page=56}}</ref>
=== Shrine Associated with Victory === Due to the legend of Omatsu's direct petitioning and subsequent defeat of evil, the shrine is believed to bring success in competitions. In recent years, this belief has extended to students praying for success in exams, particularly during the [[university entrance examination]] season. Amid [[Japan's Lost Decades]], the shrine has seen increased visits from job seekers and those praying for financial success. The shrine also accepts mail-in prayer requests for those unable to visit in person.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen (Anan City) and Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City) Attract Students|publisher=Tokushima Shimbun|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /> Uniquely, the shrine features cat-shaped [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (guardian statues).
== Timeline == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] – Shrine founded. * Early [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] – The enshrined deity's name was changed from Giri Daigongen to Omatsu Daigongen. * 1963 – Main shrine relocated to its current location.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omatsu Daigongen: Cat God for Success and Victory |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |accessdate=2023-02-22}}</ref>
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" !Event<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=Festivals|publisher=Omatsu Daigongen|accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!Date<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |New Year Festival |January 1–3 |- |Omatsu's Memorial Day |March 15 (lunar calendar) |- |Grand Festival |May 1 |- |Monthly Festival |1st and 15th of every month |}

The headman’s wife, Omatsu, petitioned the magistrate’s office but faced an unjust ruling due to the merchant’s bribery. When she appealed directly to the domain lord, Omatsu was executed for the act of direct petitioning. According to legend, her pet calico cat transformed into a [[:en:Bakeneko|Bakeneko]] (monster cat) and destroyed the homes of the merchant and the magistrate.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/yuisyo.html |title=Omatsu Gongen Shrine History |publisher=Omatsu Daigongen |accessdate=2017-01-01 |url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会">{{Cite web|url=http://www.anan-kankou.jp/bunya/omatudaigongen.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="情報サイト">{{Cite web|url=http://www.awanavi.jp/spot/2013032503003/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Awa Navi Tokushima Tourism Info |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref><ref name="阿南市">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700061/|title=Omatsu Daigongen|publisher=Anan City |accessdate=2014-4-5}}</ref>
The exact cause of Omatsu’s death varies among legends. Some say only Omatsu was executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Awa’s Stubborn People |publisher=Awa Historical Fiction Society |date=2021 |page=190}}</ref> others suggest both Omatsu and her cat were executed,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tales of Kamotani’s Past |editor=Kamotani Local History Research Association |page=37}}</ref> or that Omatsu committed suicide.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Legends of Anan |publisher=Koyama Bookstore |date=1976 |page=56}}</ref>
=== Shrine Associated with Victory === Due to the legend of Omatsu's direct petitioning and subsequent defeat of evil, the shrine is believed to bring success in competitions. In recent years, this belief has extended to students praying for success in exams, particularly during the [[university entrance examination]] season. Amid [[Japan's Lost Decades]], the shrine has seen increased visits from job seekers and those praying for financial success. The shrine also accepts mail-in prayer requests for those unable to visit in person.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8">{{Cite web|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/localNews/news/2012/01/2012_13260017706.html|title=Omatsu Daigongen (Anan City) and Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City) Attract Students|publisher=Tokushima Shimbun|accessdate=2014-04-05|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="観光協会" /><ref name="情報サイト" /><ref name="阿南市" /> Uniquely, the shrine features cat-shaped [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (guardian statues).
== Timeline == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] – Shrine founded. * Early [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] – The enshrined deity's name was changed from Giri Daigongen to Omatsu Daigongen. * 1963 – Main shrine relocated to its current location.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omatsu Daigongen: Cat God for Success and Victory |url=https://nekogami.jp/gosaijin.html |accessdate=2023-02-22}}</ref>
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" !Event<ref name="ご祭典">{{Cite web|url=http://wwwa.pikara.ne.jp/omatudaigongen/saiten.html|title=Festivals|publisher=Omatsu Daigongen|accessdate=2017-1-1}}</ref>!!Date<ref name="ご祭典"/> |- |New Year Festival |January 1–3 |- |Omatsu's Memorial Day |March 15 (lunar calendar) |- |Grand Festival |May 1 |- |Monthly Festival |1st and 15th of every month |}
== Shrine Precincts == The precincts are adorned with cat-related objects and buildings. Over 10,000 maneki-neko (beckoning cat statues) have been dedicated and can be borrowed and taken home.<ref name="徳島新聞 2012.1.8"/><ref name="観光協会"/> Notable features include: * Maneki-neko * Prayer ema * Rubbing cat statues * Archives * Giant cat statue
<gallery> File:Omatsugongen Archives.JPG|Maneki-neko File:Omatsugongen prayer No,2.JPG|Prayer ema </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]] * {{ill|Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|王子神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – Also enshrines Omatsu and her beloved cat. * {{ill|Bakeneko#Saga of the Nabeshima Bakeneko|鍋島の化け猫騒動|lt=Nabeshima Bakeneko Incident|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakeneko#Saga of the Nabeshima Bakeneko|qq=}} * {{ill|Yoriyoshi Arima|ja|有馬頼義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoriyoshi Arima|1=Yorichika Arima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516850}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Omatsugongen}} * [https://nekogami.jp/ Omatsu Daigongen Official Website]
